stem: The daily high temperatures, in degrees Fahrenheit, for a week were $62, 68, 71, 65, 74, 70, 69$. What is the range of the temperatures?
explanation: Range equals the maximum minus the minimum: $74 - 62 = 12$.

stem: A data set has minimum value $14$ and maximum value $47$. What is the range of the data set?
explanation: Range is max minus min: $47 - 14 = 33$. The sum $61$ is a common trap.

stem: Data Set X: $\{5, 5, 5, 5, 5\}$. Data Set Y: $\{1, 3, 5, 7, 9\}$. Which statement correctly compares the standard deviations of the two data sets?

choices.text: Set Y has a larger standard deviation than Set X.
Set X has a larger standard deviation than Set Y.
Set X and Set Y have equal standard deviations.
The standard deviations cannot be compared without a calculator.

explanation: Set X has no variability (all values equal, so SD $=0$), while Set Y spreads from $1$ to $9$, giving a positive standard deviation.

stem: For a data set with first quartile $Q_1 = 18$ and third quartile $Q_3 = 31$, what is the interquartile range?
explanation: The IQR is defined as $Q_3 - Q_1 = 31 - 18 = 13$.

stem: A student records the number of books read by each member of a club: $2, 4, 4, 5, 6, 8$. Which measure describes the spread of this data set?

choices.text: The mean, which is approximately $4.83$.
The median, which is $4.5$.
The mode, which is $4$.
The range, which is $6$.

explanation: Mean, median, and mode are measures of center. Only range (max $-$ min $= 8 - 2 = 6$) measures spread.

stem: Two classes took the same quiz. Class A's scores were $\{78, 80, 82, 80, 80\}$ and Class B's scores were $\{60, 75, 82, 88, 95\}$. Both classes have the same mean score of $80$. Which statement correctly compares the standard deviations of the two classes?

choices.text: Class A has the larger standard deviation because it has more values near $80$.
The two classes have equal standard deviations because they share the same mean.
Standard deviation cannot be compared when sample sizes are equal.
Class B has the larger standard deviation because its scores are more spread out from the mean.

explanation: Standard deviation measures typical distance from the mean. Class B's scores deviate much further from $80$ than Class A's tightly-clustered scores, so Class B has the larger SD.

stem: The data set $\{12, 14, 15, 17, 19, 21, 100\}$ contains an obvious outlier at $100$. Compared to the original data set, how do the range and interquartile range change if the outlier is removed?

choices.text: Both the range and the IQR decrease substantially.
The IQR decreases substantially, but the range changes very little.
The range decreases substantially, but the IQR changes very little.
Neither the range nor the IQR changes.

explanation: Range depends on extremes, so losing $100$ drops it from $88$ to $9$. The IQR (middle $50\%$) is resistant to outliers and barely shifts.

stem: Every value in a data set is increased by exactly $7$. Which statement about the standard deviation of the new data set is true?

choices.text: The standard deviation stays the same.
The standard deviation increases by $7$.
The standard deviation is multiplied by $7$.
The standard deviation becomes $0$.

explanation: Adding a constant shifts every value the same amount, so distances from the mean are unchanged. Spread is unaffected by translation.

stem: A data set of nine values, ordered from least to greatest, is $\{3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 19\}$. Using the method that excludes the overall median when computing quartiles, what is the interquartile range?
explanation: With nine values, the overall median is $11$. Excluding it, $Q_1$ is the median of $\{3,5,7,9\}$, which is $6$, and $Q_3$ is the median of $\{13,15,17,19\}$, which is $16$. IQR $= 16 - 6 = 10$.

stem: Two data sets each contain $50$ values. Data Set P has values tightly clustered between $48$ and $52$. Data Set Q has values spread roughly evenly between $10$ and $90$. Both have a mean of $50$. Which data set has the larger standard deviation, and why?

choices.text: Set P, because it has a smaller range and therefore more precise values.
Set Q, because its values are typically farther from the mean of $50$.
They are equal, because both have the same mean of $50$.
Set P, because tightly clustered data always has a larger SD.

explanation: Standard deviation measures the typical distance between each value and the mean. Set Q's values sit much farther from $50$ on average than Set P's, so Q's SD is larger.

stem: A researcher reports that the standard deviation of a data set is $0$. Which of the following must be true?

choices.text: Every value in the data set is equal.
The mean of the data set is $0$.
The data set contains exactly one value.
The range of the data set is undefined.

explanation: Standard deviation is $0$ only when every value is identical (each value has zero distance from the mean).

stem: A data set has interquartile range $12$ and median $40$. A statistician adds the single new value $41$, which lies between the old $Q_1$ and $Q_3$. How will the IQR most directly be affected?

choices.text: It will change very little, if at all.
It will increase substantially.
It will decrease substantially.
It will double, since one extra value was added.

explanation: Adding a single value near the middle barely shifts the quartiles, so the IQR is essentially unchanged.

stem: For the data set $\{4, 7, 9, 12, 15, 18, 22\}$, what is the range?
explanation: Range equals the maximum minus the minimum: $22 - 4 = 18$.

stem: The ordered data set $\{2, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 17\}$ has eight values. What is its interquartile range?
explanation: The lower half is $\{2,5,6,8\}$, giving $Q_1 = 5.5$. The upper half is $\{10,12,14,17\}$, giving $Q_3 = 13$. IQR $= 13 - 5.5 = 7.5$.

stem: Which of the following changes to a data set would have the LARGEST effect on the standard deviation but the SMALLEST effect on the interquartile range?

choices.text: Shifting every value upward by the same constant.
Adding a single new value that is far above the maximum.
Replacing the median with a value very close to it.
Multiplying every value by $1$.

explanation: Extreme values dramatically pull the mean and increase deviations (affecting SD), but they lie outside the middle $50\%$, so the IQR barely moves.

stem: Data set $S$ has standard deviation $\sigma$. Every value in $S$ is then multiplied by $3$ to produce data set $S'$. What is the standard deviation of $S'$ in terms of $\sigma$?

choices.text: $\sigma$
$9\sigma$
$3\sigma$
$\sigma + 3$

explanation: Multiplying each value by a constant $k$ scales distances from the mean by $|k|$, so the standard deviation is multiplied by $3$. Variance — not SD — would be multiplied by $9$.

stem: Data Set $A$ has $100$ values symmetrically spread between $20$ and $80$ with mean $50$. Data Set $B$ has $100$ values with $98$ of them equal to $50$, one value equal to $0$, and one value equal to $100$; it also has mean $50$. Which of the following statements must be true?

choices.text: Both $A$ and $B$ have IQR equal to $0$.
$B$ has a larger IQR than $A$ because it contains the extreme values $0$ and $100$.
The IQRs of $A$ and $B$ must be equal because both sets have the same mean.
The interquartile range of $B$ is $0$, while $A$ has a much larger IQR.

explanation: In $B$, the middle $50\%$ of values are all $50$, so $Q_3 - Q_1 = 0$. Set $A$'s values are spread across $[20,80]$, so its middle $50\%$ — and therefore its IQR — is clearly much larger.

stem: A data set has mean $\mu$ and standard deviation $\sigma > 0$. A new data point equal to $\mu$ is added to the set. Which statement about the new standard deviation is true?

choices.text: It is equal to $\sigma$.
It is strictly greater than $\sigma$.
It is strictly less than $\sigma$.
It is equal to $0$.

explanation: Adding a value equal to the mean leaves the mean unchanged and contributes zero to the sum of squared deviations. Dividing that same total by a larger $n$ reduces the variance — and therefore the standard deviation — so the new SD is smaller.

stem: Data Set $U$ consists of the ten values $\{10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 20, 20, 20, 20, 20\}$. Data Set $V$ consists of the ten values $\{5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25\}$. Both have mean $15$. Which statement correctly compares the standard deviations?

choices.text: $U$ has the larger standard deviation because its values are closer together.
$U$ has the larger standard deviation because it has more values above $15$.
$V$ has the larger standard deviation because its values are farther from $15$.
The two sets have equal standard deviations because they share the same mean and sample size.

explanation: In $U$ each value is $5$ away from the mean; in $V$ each value is $10$ away. Larger typical distance from the mean means larger standard deviation, so $V > U$.

stem: A data set of $20$ values has range $40$ and IQR $10$. A statistician removes the single largest value and the single smallest value. Which statement about the resulting data set of $18$ values must be true?

choices.text: The new range is exactly $40$.
The new IQR is exactly $10$.
The new range is at most $40$.
The new IQR is exactly $0$.

explanation: Removing the extremes can only shrink or preserve the range, so it is at most the original $40$. IQR behavior depends on where the new $Q_1$ and $Q_3$ land and isn't guaranteed to remain exactly $10$.
